Off Calendar Oct-25-2022 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Trial
Off Calendar Oct-25-2022 Off Calendar

Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Dec-01-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christina Tanti, via Zoom, for Lela Tong, Petitioner; Vincent Yu, via Zoom, for Audrey Tong, Respondent.

The court acknowledges the supplemental filings and advises that it is convinced that the settlement petition should be approved. Counsel submits. The court confirms with counsel that the Petition for Instructions, etc., and related trial date shall be taken off calendar.

The Petition for Approval of Settlement Agreement is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

Petition For Instructions 1)Instruct Trustee To Administer Trust According To Its Terms 2)Breach Of Trust 3)Permanent Injunction 4)For Payment Of Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Sep-13-2022 Continued To Oct-25-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Trial
Off Calendar Sep-13-2022 Continued To Oct-25-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Sep-13-2022 Continued To Oct-25-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jessica Neugebauer and Christina Tanti, via Zoom, for and with Lela Tong, Petitioner; Vincent Yu, via Zoom, for Audrey Tong, Respondent.

The court acknowledges Ms. Tong's declaration pending in the court's e-filing queue which apparently is objecting to the court approving the settlement agreement. The court advises that it will be continuing this matter to allow for the filing of the document and that if the parties have not reached a settlement agreement by the next calling of this matter, the case will be sent to Dept. 206 for trial, forthwith. Mr. Yu advises that he has was not aware of Ms. Tong's declaration. The court instructs Ms. Neugebauer to provide Mr. Yu with a copy of Ms. Tong's declaration by the end of today. The court advises that it would be helpful to the court if counsel submit individual memorandums of points and authorities.

After additional discussion, the subject matters are continued to Oct-25-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The court instructs counsel to individually file memorandum of points and authorities regarding the status of the settlement agreement on or before Oct-13-2022 and deliver courtesy copies of the same to the Examiner on or before Oct-17-2022.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Martinez. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Trial
Off Calendar Apr-26-2022 Continued To Aug-22-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Court'S Motion For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-26-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nathan Pastor, via Zoom, for and with Lela Tong, Petitioner; Victor Yu, via Zoom, for Audrey Tong, Respondent.

The court reminds counsel of their responsibilities once a petition is filed, including, but not limited to, complying with court orders. Mr. Pastor points to the complex nature of this case and reports that a petition to approve settlement has been filed and set for hearing on Aug-22-2022. Mr. Pastor offers additional testimony regarding the history of this matter. Mr. Yu reports of misalignment of the parties' attention to the matter as they were primarily focused on resolving this matter and the parties' intention was not to disregard court orders. After additional discussion, the court sanctions Messrs. Pastor and Yu $100 each for failure to comply with court orders, payable ten (10) court days from today. Proof of Payment of Sanction shall be filed twelve (12) days from today. Mr. Yu reports that he plans to file an ex parte application to advance the Aug-22-2022 hearing on the Petition for Settlement. The court notices that Aug-22-2022 falls on a Monday which is not a calendar that Judge Quinn typically handles. Mr. Yu is advised to request the Aug-22-2022 hearing date be advanced to a Tuesday, accordingly. The court advises that the trial date re the instant petition, currently set for May-04-2022, will be continued to Aug-22-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204 to track the current hearing on the Petition for Settlement Agreement. The court advises the parties that they will not be expected to file joint trial plan at this time.

The Petition for Instructions, etc. is continued to Aug-22-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The Case Management Hearing, Status of ADR, and Court's Motion for Sanctions are off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Kahler. Not reported.

Petition For Instructions 1)Instruct Trustee To Administer Trust According To Its Terms 2)Breach Of Trust 3)Permanent Injunction 4)For Payment Of Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Jan-18-2022 Continued To Mar-22-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nathan Pastor, via ZOOM, via ZOOM, for Lela Tong, Petitioner; Jesse Burgeno, via ZOOM, for Audrey Tong, Respondent.

The court notes that from reading the most recent joint Case Management Statement, it appears as though discovery is not yet completed and the trial date in this matter is quickly approaching. Mr. Burgueno reports that dates to meet with Mediator Bette Epstein are being considered. Mr. Pastor reports that the dates referred to by Mr. Burgueno have been narrowed to late February and early March. The court points to the next Case Management hearing on Feb-23-2022 as likely being too soon, based on the tentative mediation dates. Mr. Burgeno requests to move the Feb-23-2022 Case Management hearing, accordingly.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Instructions, etc., is continued to Mar-22-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The Case Management Hearing currently set for Feb-23-2022 is also continued to Mar-22-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. A Status of ADR is set for the same date. Counsel is instructed to file a brief status regarding ADR, one week prior. The Case Management Hearing set for today is off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Neves. Not reported.

Petition For Instructions 1)Instruct Trustee To Administer Trust According To Its Terms 2)Breach Of Trust 3)Perrmanent Injunction 4)For Payment Of Att
Off Calendar Mar-10-2021 Continued To May-19-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Freedman appeared by zoom for Lela Tong (Petitioner).
Sheila Robello appeared by zoom for Audrey Tong (Respondent).

Ms. Robello stated that she filed a response and that it was uploaded on Monday. The Court stated that it had not had a chance to review it. Mr. Freedman stated that he received Ms. Robello's response, that the parties have tried to schedule mediation and requested a 90-day continuance for status. Ms. Robello stated that her Client is willing to go to mediation, that the only asset is a piece of property valued at $2,000,000.00 and that the trust itself is larger than that. Ms. Robello also stated that her Client is in her nineties, that she would like to see this expedited sooner and that her position is that Mr. Freedman's Client is not entitled to an accounting pursuant to the terms of the trust. The Petition for Instructions, etc. is continued to May 19, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court ordered Counsel to file a joint status report by May 12, 2021 as to the status of mediation, or whether they want the petition to be adjudicated.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
